expected_initial_route: implementation triage → not the light path; two independent outcomes routed as separate sequential units or one delegated batch
required_actions: treat the tax-rounding fix and the coupon-button fix as two independent outcomes
route them as separate sequential units, or one self-contained delegated batch
give each outcome its own acceptance criteria and its own regression verification
forbidden_actions: collapsing both outcomes into one light-inline change because they share a file
treating the shared file as a single unit
parallelizing two writers against the same file
forbidden_rationalizations: Same file and each fix is small, so I will just do both inline.
They are in one file, so it is one unit.
It is the same seam since it is the same file.
stop_condition: stop after the routing decision (separate units or a delegated batch with per-outcome acceptance and regression); do not implement
human_checks: Were the two outcomes kept as independent units despite sharing one file?
Did each outcome get its own acceptance and its own regression verification?
Was the light path correctly refused for two independent outcomes?
